topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions or reductions of duties on various imported goods as outlined in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on light emitting diode (LED) spot light modules
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain rolling backpacks
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain switchgear assemblies for wind turbine generators
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain acrylic staple fibers
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: reduce temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Imidacloprid and Cyfluthrin
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on benzene, 1,3-diisocyanatomethyl-
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic staple fibers
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on programmable controllers
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 9: suspend the duty on Cyprosulfamide
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 10: extend the temporary suspension of duty on cerium sulfide pigments
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Affordable Housing and Economic Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving affordable housing access and economic development, particularly for low-income communities and vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act of 2009 - States that any immediate successor in interest to residential property in foreclosure assumes such interest subject to...
giving an existing tenant at least 90-day notice to vacate; and...
Document 2: Amends the Housing Act of 1949, with respect to financial assistance to farm owners...
to consider as rural or a rural area for farm housing purposes a place, town, village, or city with a population of 35,000 or less...
Document 3: Small Public Housing Authority Act - Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to exempt a small public housing agency from the requirement to prepare an annual public agency plan...
if the agency administers not more than a total of 250 dwelling units and section 8 vouchers...
Document 4: Securing Equity for the Economic Development of Low Income Areas Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration...
to select at least one company from each geographic region of the SBA.
Document 5: Small Business Contracting Programs Parity Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Act...
to authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses...
Document 6: Increasing Small Business Lending Act of 2011 - Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend small business loan fee reductions...
Makes uniform between the Small Business Administration (SBA) section 7(a) loan program and the SBA local development business loan program the office space leasing policy...
Document 8: Responsible GSE Affordable Housing Investment Act of 2010 - Amends the Federal Housing Enterprises Financial Safety and Soundness Act of 1992 to revise requirements for determination of compliance with the multifamily special affordable housing goal.
Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to establish standards for the multifamily special affordable housing goal...
Document 10: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements under the HOPE for Homeowners Program...
Revises the requirement of certification to the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) that the mortgagor has not intentionally defaulted on the existing mortgage...

topic.name: Consumer Privacy and Protection Laws
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer privacy and protection in telecommunications and digital communications.

Document 1: Smartphone Theft Prevention Act of 2015
protect consumers
Document 2: Protecting Consumer Phone Records Act
civil and criminal penalties for violations
Document 3: Do-Not-Call Registry Fee Extension Act
require the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to collect an annual fee
Document 4: Email Privacy Act
prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service
Document 6: Geolocational Privacy and Surveillance Act
prohibit intentionally intercepting geolocation information
Document 7: Do Not Track Kids Act of 2013
additional privacy protections against the collection of personal information from children
Document 8: Cell Phone Consumer Empowerment Act of 2007
disclose contract information regarding terms, charges, minutes
Document 9: Child Pornography Elimination Act of 2007
increase fines for the intentional failure of Internet service providers to report online child pornography

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming and improving various aspects of the education system in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to prohibit the federal government from directly or indirectly mandating...
Prohibits the Secretary of Education from conditioning the provision of a statutory or regulatory waiver under the ESEA...
Document 2: This bill declares that it shall not be unlawful under federal law for a state or local government to enact or enforce a policy regarding the use of sex-segregated bathrooms...
Document 3: Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act's program of grants to states...
Ensure that homeless youth do not lose credits earned in other schools...
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award up to five grants to partnerships of local educational agencies...
Designed to enhance and assess the biliteracy and bilingualism skills of low-income minority and limited English proficient children...
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise and augment programs created or amended under the No Child Left Behind Act of 2001.
Requires states to implement data systems capable of tracking individual student performance over time...
Document 6: Amends the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act to require states to use grants for safe and drug-free schools...
Establish grievance procedures for students and parents to register complaints regarding such conduct.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow local educational agencies (LEAs) that are unable to provide a small, rural, or remote school...
Gives schools which are identified as needing improvement an extra year to make AYP...
Document 8: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to make matching grants...
Includes school counselors, school psychologists or other psychologists, child or adolescent psychiatrists, and school social workers among such providers.
Document 9: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require local educational agencies receiving subgrants...
Create a network of schools or programs of study that integrate a rigorous curriculum emphasizing college and career readiness...
Document 10: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to condition local educational agency (LEA) receipt of school improvement funds...
Requires annual LEA report cards to include certain information on state and local spending per pupil in schools.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Special Accounts
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that provide tax benefits and credits related to specific savings accounts and financial assistance for individuals and families.

Document 1: amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the tax deduction for dividends received
reduce such deduction for corporations that do not maintain certain employment levels
Document 2: 401Kids Family Savings Act of 2013
allow the use of such accounts to pay the acquisition costs of a first-time homebuyer
Document 3: Hearing Aid Assistance Tax Credit Act 2011
nonrefundable income tax credit of up to $500 for the purchase of a qualified hearing aid
Document 4: ABLE to Work Act of 2016
allow an ABLE account beneficiary to make contributions to an ABLE account
Document 5: Fair Taxes for Seniors Act of 2007
increase in the exclusion of gain from the sale of a principal residence
Document 6: amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the dollar limitation on employment-related expenses
make such credit refundable
Document 7: Municipal Bond Market Support Act of 2015
permanently increase from $10 million to $30 million the annual limit on tax-exempt obligations
Document 8: Tax Free Tips Act of 2011
exempt tips from the income tax, social security and railroad retirement taxes
Document 9: Child Health Care Affordability Act
allow a tax credit for the medical expenses of a dependent
Document 10: Small Business Jobs and Tax Relief Act
allow certain employers a tax credit for 10% of the excess wages and compensation paid

topic.name: Environmental Protection and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation, management, and protection of natural resources and environmental quality in the United States.

Document 9: Requires the President to establish the Task Force on Super Pollutants to review existing and potential policies and measures that promote the reduction of short-lived climate pollutants
identify, compile, evaluate, and develop best practices for reducing short-lived climate pollutants.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Tax Incentives
topic.description: The documents discuss various tax incentives and credits related to renewable energy production, energy efficiency, and alternative fuels in the United States.

Document 1: residential energy efficient property tax credit for 30% of expenditures
financing of qualified distributed thermal energy storage property
Document 2: income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel used as fuel
excise tax credit for alternative fuels
Document 3: tax credit for purchasing a new qualified hybrid motor vehicle
tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property
Document 4: tax incentives for renewable energy and other energy-related tax incentives
tax credit for investment in solar energy and qualified fuel cell property
Document 5: 30% energy tax credit for investment in qualified advanced biofuel production property
grant in lieu of a tax credit for investment in such property
Document 6: tax credit for the production of renewable chemicals
produced in the United States from renewable biomass
Document 7: excise tax on the carbon content of any taxable fuel
annual increases in the amount of such tax
Document 8: increase to $1,500 the maximum dollar amount of the credit for fuel cell property expenditures
fuel cell property eligible for such credit
Document 9: reduction in the tax credit for the production of electricity from renewable resources
exempt certain loan guarantees or grants
Document 10: extend certain tax incentives for energy production and conservation
tax credit for the production of electricity from renewable resources

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing the welfare, protection, and legal rights of children, particularly in contexts of abuse, neglect, and family separation.

Document 1: providing child welfare agencies and community organizations access to detained individuals to help DHS identify detainees who have children
requiring that the interests of children be considered in decisions regarding detainee release, detention, or transfer.
Document 2: require states receiving Edward Byrne Memorial justice assistance grants to certify the implementation of a policy for requiring all rape kits to be sent to crime laboratories for forensic analysis
provide for sexual assault forensic medical personnel examiners to collect and preserve evidence, provide expert testimony, and provide treatment of trauma relating to sexual assault.
Document 3: authorize the Secretary to award grants to public or private agencies and organizations to develop or expand effective collaborations between child protective service entities and domestic violence service entities

direct the Secretary to study and report to Congress on efforts to coordinate the objectives and activities of agencies and organizations responsible for programs and activities related to child abuse and neglect.
Document 6: direct the Department of Health and Human Services to award regional and state partnership grants to address the substance use needs of families in the child welfare system
require state child welfare agencies to have in effect a protocol for coordinating substance abuse prevention and treatment services with child welfare services.
Document 8: require a state plan for its child protective services system to include a certified assurance that the state has in effect and is enforcing a law requiring identification and assessment of all reports involving children known or suspected to be victims of sex trafficking
training child protective services workers about identifying, assessing, and providing comprehensive services for children who are sex trafficking victims.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the rights, protections, and benefits of veterans and servicemembers.

1: requires an adjudication by a federal or state court before a veteran can be prohibited from owning a firearm based on mental health determinations.
2: extends the period of protection for a servicemember against mortgage sale or foreclosure after military service.
4: appropriates funds for military construction, family housing, and base realignment.
5: authorizes the burial in a national cemetery of a parent of a deceased veteran.
8: establishes the American Veterans Congressional Internship Program.
10: waives the basic military pay adjustment and increases military pay.

topic.name: Workplace and Educational Rights
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at protecting the rights of individuals in educational and employment settings, focusing on non-discrimination, workplace safety, and accommodations for specific groups.

Document 1: Prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating in... on the basis of their actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Prohibits retaliation against anyone for opposing conduct they reasonably believe to be unlawful under this Act.
Document 2: Declares it an unlawful employment practice for employers... to not make reasonable accommodations to known limitations related to the pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.
Sets forth enforcement procedures and remedies under the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
Document 4: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Sets forth increased protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 5: Entitles veterans... to leave from such employer on Veterans Day upon request if such veteran would otherwise be required to work on Veterans Day.
Prohibits... employers from interfering with, restraining, or denying the taking of any leave provided under this Act.
Document 10: Prohibits discharging or retaliating against any employee because such employee has inquired about, discussed, or disclosed comparative compensation information.
Makes such prohibition inapplicable to instances in which an employee who has access to the wage information of other employees... discloses the wages of such other employees.

topic.name: Aviation and Communication Regulations
topic.description: This cluster addresses regulations and studies related to aviation safety, passenger rights, and communication practices.

Document 1: prohibiting commercial flight crew members from using portable electronic devices in the aircraft's cockpit
study and report to Congress on common sources of distraction for cockpit commercial flight crew members
Document 2: independent study of the effects on human health caused by the use of backscatter x-ray machines
ensure large, readable signs at airline passenger checkpoints
Document 3: revise the do-not-call registry provisions to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls
defines politically-oriented recorded message telephone call
Document 4: amend regulations concerning the height and dimensions of station antenna structures
prohibit a private land use restriction from applying to amateur service communications
Document 5: provide additional unlicensed spectrum in the 5850-5925 megahertz band
technical rules suitable for the widespread commercial development of unlicensed operations
Document 6: display airline ticket price without simultaneously displaying all applicable ticket taxes, fees, charges, and fuel surcharges
Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations to carry out this Act
Document 7: requires each air carrier and airport operator to submit for approval a proposed contingency plan
provide passengers on a departure- or arrival-delayed grounded aircraft with adequate food, water, restrooms
Document 8: requires the general notice of proposed rulemaking to include any scientific research relevant to the rulemaking
defines scientific study for purposes of the rulemaking process
Document 9: require the Secretary of Transportation to prohibit the operator of an unmanned recreational aircraft from flying it above 400 feet
within 5 miles of the perimeter of a commercial airport
Document 10: require each satellite carrier to carry, on request, the signals of all television broadcast stations
subject to existing broadcast signal retransmission consent provisions

topic.name: Land and Resource Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to the management, transfer, and conservation of land and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to cancel Bureau of Land Management mineral contracts
prohibit mining in such area.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to convey to the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium specified property
for use in connection with health and related programs.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary concerned to treat a qualified local government as if such government is a federal agency
for purposes of an analysis under the National Environmental Policy Act.
Document 4: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior
to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the planning, design, and construction of the Riverside-Corona Feeder water supply project
which includes groundwater wells and water storage facilities.
Document 6: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration to approve a request from the town of Pollock, Louisiana, to close the municipal airport
and release the town from any restrictions.
Document 7: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management land in Oregon as wilderness
and withdraws federal land within the wilderness areas from all forms of entry.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the state of California a parcel of National Forest System land
to permit the state to use such land to relocate the South Operations Coordination Center.
Document 9: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey to the municipality of Anchorage, Alaska
the reversionary interests of the United States in specified sites.
Document 10: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease
for the exploration, development, or production of oil, natural gas, or any other mineral.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing oversight and security measures within federal agencies and their operations.

Document 1: directs the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to assess the level of risk posed to the domestic air transportation system
The TSA shall issue guidance to airport operators for placing an expiration date on each airport credential
Document 2: Requires each executive agency to establish and maintain specified safeguards and internal controls for official purchase cards
Requires the guidance to direct each agency head with more than $10 million in purchase card spending annually
Document 3: Amends the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) to revise requirements for federal agency disclosures
Directs the Attorney General to notify the Special Counsel of civil actions taken for arbitrary and capricious rejections
Document 4: requires the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) to post on its website detailed transition plans
Conditions a federal entity's eligibility to receive payment for relocations costs on an Office of Management and Budget finding
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used
Document 6: empowers the Inspector General to refer to the lead agency a potential covered case for suspension or debarment
Defines a 'covered case' as one involving a person that is an Afghan national or foreign national
Document 7: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from issuing a notice of proposed rulemaking unless it provides at least 30 days for comments
Requires such notice to include an identification of a notice of inquiry
Document 8: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to require Inspectors General for designated federal entities to be appointed without regard to political affiliation
Prohibits IGs from receiving cash awards or bonuses
Document 9: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2012
Document 10: amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to exempt inspectors general from information privacy protections
CIGIE must submit to Congress an annual report that it currently submits only to the President

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts focused on the management, protection, and restoration of water resources and ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Renews the Water Resources Development Act (WRDA).
Authorizes Army Corps of Engineers (Corps) water resources development and conservation projects.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to submit to Congress a financial report containing restoration activities.
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to develop and update biennially an adaptive management plan for restoration activities.

topic.name: Clean Energy and Climate Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to clean energy, greenhouse gas emissions reduction, and the promotion of renewable energy sources.

Document 1: provisions concerning the reduction of global warming pollution
require regulation for limiting and reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions
Document 2: authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline
deems the Final Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement regarding the pipeline
Document 5: provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions
Document 9: Capping Greenhouse Gas Emissions
requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments and acts related to trade, agriculture, transportation, and public safety in the United States.

Document 1: permanent authority of Washington, Oregon, and California to adopt and enforce state laws governing fishing and processing
exclusive economic zone adjacent to their state in any Dungeness crab fishery
Document 2: prohibit the Secretary of Commerce from providing more than 50% of the costs
report to Congress on cost share requirements
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain ice cream makers
Document 4: eliminate the 15-year limitation on the period for which borrowers of agricultural operating loans are eligible
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on certain tamper resistant ground fault circuit interrupters
Document 6: allows railroad employees to remain or go on duty for a period in excess of limitations
clear a blockage of vehicular traffic at a grade crossing
Document 7: extends and modifies the temporary suspension of duty on certain men's footwear
Document 8: make provisions concerning commercial aviation accidents applicable to maritime accidents
Document 9: make grants to assist states with costs associated with DNA collection processes
awards bonus payments to states that have implemented enhanced DNA collection
Document 10: authorize appropriations for the U.S. Anti-Doping Agency
preventing the use of prohibited performance-enhancing methods

topic.name: Social Security and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments related to Social Security and Medicare, focusing on benefits, eligibility, and adjustments for individuals and families.

Document 1: Remarried Widows' and Widowers' Benefits Act of 2007
entitled to widow's or widower's insurance benefits
Document 2: Cost of Living Act
adjustment of the percentage increase in OASDI benefits
Document 3: Defend and Save Social Security Act
increase the normal retirement age
Document 4: limit Medicare Advantage payment rate increases
achieve parity with the Medicare fee-for-service capitation rate
Document 5: Seniors' Health Care Choice Act of 2015
establish a special enrollment period under Medicare part B
Document 6: Comprehensive Immunosuppressive Drug Coverage for Kidney Transplant Patients Act of 2013
extend the months of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs
Document 7: require survivor annuity payments made to blind or disabled dependents
disregarded in eligibility and benefit determinations
Document 8: Veterans' Survivors Claims Processing Automation Act of 2015
pay burial and funeral expenses
Document 9: Government Pension Offset Reform Act
modify the formula for determining the amount of reduced monthly OASDI benefits
Document 10: Social Security and Medicare Improved Burn Injury Treatment Access Act of 2007
waive the application of the five-month Social Security disability waiting period

topic.name: Education and Workforce Development
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational resources, workforce training, and support for specific populations in various fields.

Document 1: Student-to-school nurse ratio improvement
grants to reduce such ratio
Document 2: grants to wildlife or veterinary institutions
create positions in areas of need
Document 3: health and education grant program
support activities by nongovernmental organizations
Document 4: fund scholarships for students majoring in nuclear science
developing a robust nuclear workforce
Document 5: demonstration grants to reduce the student-to-nurse ratio
eligible local education agency
Document 6: Expanded Learning and After-School Fellows program
support exemplary recent college graduates
Document 7: establish a master's or doctoral degree program in orthotics and prosthetics
improve orthotic and prosthetic outcomes for veterans
Document 8: awarding grants to research and development entities
encourage the replication of innovations
Document 9: awarding grants to local educational agencies for school counseling programs
engage students in manufacturing careers
Document 10: establish a nanotechnology in the schools program
provision of nanotechnology education to students

topic.name: Federal Research and Innovation Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing federal research, innovation, and management practices across various sectors.

Document 1: requires the National Science Foundation (NSF) to maintain the intellectual merit and broader impacts criteria as the basis for evaluating grant proposals
NIST shall research information systems for future cybersecurity needs.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to establish Federal Executive Boards
develop local coordinated approaches to the development and operation of programs that have common characteristics.
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to award grants to institutions of higher education
establishes the National Innovation Council in the Executive Office of the President to coordinate federal innovation policy.
Document 4: requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to develop a policy for federal science agencies
requires federal science agencies to collect specified standardized annual data for all applications for research and development grants.
Document 5: adopt and oversee government-wide standards, policies, and guidelines for program and project management for executive agencies
establish a five-year strategic plan for program and project management.
Document 6: directs the National Science Foundation (NSF) to maintain a Large Facilities Office to support its research directorates
requires an external cost proposal analysis for any major multi-user research facility.
Document 7: develop resources to train Native American and Alaska Native tribal council members in self-government
provide educational outreach regarding tribal self-government.
Document 8: reauthorizes the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases the set-aside allocation under the STTR from 0.3% to 0.6%.
Document 9: requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to develop performance measures to determine whether federal agencies are providing high quality customer service
requires the head of each agency to collect information from its customers regarding the quality of its customer services.
Document 10: reauthorizes the SCORE program for Small Business Administration (SBA) grants
directs the SBA to ensure that the SCORE program and each of its chapters develop and implement plans and goals to more effectively and efficiently provide services.

topic.name: Federal Energy Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to the exploration, production, and management of federal energy resources, particularly oil, gas, and geothermal energy.

Document 1: Study of Ways to Improve the Accuracy of the Collection of Federal Oil, Condensate, and Natural Gas Royalties Act of 2011
accuracy of collection of royalties on production of oil, condensate, and natural gas under leases of federal lands
Document 2: Energy Exploration and Production to Achieve National Demand Act or EXPAND Act
amends the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006 to repeal the moratorium upon oil and gas leasing
Document 3: Fixing America's Inequities with Revenues Act of 2013 or FAIR Act of 2013
direct the Secretary of the Interior to deposit into a special account in the Treasury 37.5% of all revenues payable to the United States from oil, natural gas, and alternative and renewable energy
Document 4: Offshore Petroleum Expansion Now Act of 2012
directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the Proposed Oil & Gas Leasing Program: 2012-2017
Document 5: Exploring for Geothermal Energy on Federal Lands Act
exempts projects determined by the Secretary of the Interior to be geothermal exploration test projects from environmental impact statement requirements
Document 6: Drill Now Act of 2008
repeal its prohibitions against oil and natural gas preleasing and leasing activities in specified offshore areas
Document 7: Abandoned Mine Lands Cleanup and Taxpayer Fairness Act
subjects production of locatable minerals to a royalty of 12.5% of the gross income from mining
Document 8: Native American Energy Act
allows the Secretary of the Interior, an affected Indian tribe, or a certified third-party appraiser under contract with the Indian tribe to appraise Indian land or trust assets
Document 9: Bonneville Unit Clean Hydropower Facilitation Act
facilitate hydropower development on the Diamond Fork System
Document 10: authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities for the import of crude oil
deems a certain Environmental Impact Statement issued by the Secretary of State to satisfy all requirements of NEPA

topic.name: Criminal Justice Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming sentencing laws and regulations related to controlled substances and firearms.

1: retroactive application of the penalty amendments of the Fair Sentencing Act of 2010
reduce the term of imprisonment for cocaine base offenses
4: impose a sentence for specified controlled substance offenses without regard to any statutory minimum sentence
reduce mandatory minimum sentences for manufacturing, distributing, dispensing, possessing controlled substances
6: revise the civil penalties for violations of firearms law
8: revise the civil penalties for violations of firearms law
establish guidelines for ATF inspections, examinations, or investigations of possible firearms violations
10: provide that the Controlled Substances Act shall not apply to marihuana
remove marihuana from the list of Schedule I controlled substances

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on national security, immigration control, and foreign relations involving the U.S.

Document 1: designate Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps Qods Force as a foreign terrorist organization
report to Congress on Qods Force terrorist activities.
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from granting a work authorization to an alien
determined to have been at any time unlawfully present in the United States.
Document 4: Prohibits any direct U.S. assistance to the Palestinian Authority
certifies to Congress that the PA has recognized Israel's right to exist.
Document 5: extends the Afghan special immigrant visa program
expands the scope of a principal alien's qualifying employment.
Document 7: prohibits the Department of Homeland Security from admitting an alien from Syria or Iraq
until Congress passes a joint resolution authorizing DHS to resume such admissions.
Document 8: prohibits the use of federal funds to transfer to the United States any individual detained at Guantanamo Bay
a foreign national captured or detained outside the United States.
Document 9: directs the President to ensure operational control of the U.S.-Mexico border
the Border Patrol will have 23,000 full-time agents.

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving policies, funding, and support for institutions of higher education and their students.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires an IHE to include, in its annual security report provided to students and employees, a statement of its current policy on concealed carry or open carry of firearms.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants
providing eligible students with opportunity scholarships to attend the District of Columbia private elementary school or secondary school.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
to require the Secretary of Education, in calculating the cohort default rate for a proprietary institution of higher education.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require institutions of higher education (IHEs) that provide student loan counseling
to include information on creating a durable power of attorney for financial decision-making.
Document 6: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide educational and vocational counseling services for veterans
at VA-selected institutions of higher learning.
Document 9: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA)
to make students ineligible to receive William D. Ford Federal Direct Loans (DLs) on or after July 1, 2015.
Document 10: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
to require the Secretary of Education to disclose, on the form notifying students of their expected family contribution.

topic.name: U.S. Land and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, designation, and conservation of U.S. lands and resources, including national parks, historical sites, and indigenous lands.

Document 1: Conveys to the government of the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands submerged lands surrounding such Islands
Includes the Northern Mariana Islands among the islands where the President may establish naval defensive sea areas
Document 2: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to modify a land grant patent issued to the Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society
Document 3: Restoration of the U.S.-Russia Polar Bear Conservation Fund Act of 2015
This bill amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972
Document 5: Designates the North Palisade... as the Brower Palisade in honor of the late David Brower
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer specified funds previously appropriated to the National Park Service
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a study of the site of Green McAdoo School to evaluate the national significance

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts aimed at regulating environmental issues, particularly air quality and fuel standards.

Document 1: Carbon Pollution Transparency Act requires a carbon score for legislation.
Projected net greenhouse gas emissions must be calculated.
Document 2: LEVEL Act amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program.
Reduces the percentage of renewable fuel required in gasoline.
Document 3: BREATHE Act amends the Clean Air Act to aggregate emissions from oil and gas wells.
Requires the listing of hydrogen sulfide as a hazardous air pollutant.
Document 4: General Duty Clarification Act requires EPA to define terms related to hazardous air pollutants.
Modifies the definition of 'accidental release'.
Document 5: Export American Natural Gas Act directs Secretary of Energy to approve LNG export applications.
Reports on foreign natural gas resources and their export potential.
Document 6: REST Act revises regulations for hours of service for drivers at oil well sites.
Ensures rest breaks and maximum driving time for commercial vehicles.
Document 7: Amends the Clean Air Act to exclude certain gases from being classified as air pollutants.
Declares that climate change regulation is not authorized.
Document 8: Military Environmental Responsibility Act requires DOD to comply with environmental laws.
Waives U.S. immunity regarding environmental compliance.
Document 9: EPA may authorize mid-level ethanol blends if certain conditions are met.
Involves public notice and comment on the blend's impact.
Document 10: Phantom Fuels Elimination Act revises renewable fuel program requirements.
Eliminates volume standards for corn-starch ethanol.

topic.name: STEM Immigration Policies
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at modifying immigration policies specifically for individuals with advanced degrees in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 55,000 visas available to qualified immigrants who have a doctorate degree in a STEM field.
Defines 'United States doctoral institution of higher education' and eligibility criteria for visa applicants.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 10% of the worldwide employment-based immigration level available to qualified immigrants with a doctorate degree in STEM.
Reduces the number of immigrant visas available to skilled workers, professionals, and other workers.
Document 4: Exempts from the annual H-1B visa cap an alien who has earned a master's or higher degree in STEM from an institution outside of the United States.
Sets H-1B annual limits and revises provisions related to H-1B visa applications.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an annual cap on H-1B nonimmigrant visas at between 115,000 and 195,000 depending on market conditions.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to authorize the accompanying spouse of an H-1B alien to work in the United States.

topic.name: Retirement and Employee Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments and acts related to retirement plans, employee benefits, and tax credits for employers and employees.

Document 1: Makes technical amendments to the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) regarding minimum funding standards for defined benefit pension plans.
Amends the Pension Protection Act of 2006 with respect to funding of multiple employer plans of eligible charities.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, expanding the eligibility of small employers for the health insurance tax credit.
Repeals the requirement that employers make uniform contributions of at least 50% of each employee's premium to qualify for the tax credit.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) regarding guaranteed benefits for single-employer and multiemployer plans.
Requires the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation to compute the actuarial value of monthly benefits for commercial airline pilots.
Document 4: Requires the Department of the Treasury and the Social Security Administration to create an online Retirement Savings Lost and Found to assist individuals in locating employer-sponsored retirement accounts.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to set forth additional reporting and notification requirements for retirement plans.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code regarding the tax treatment of church pension plans.
Allows church plans and their supporting organizations to invest plan assets in a group trust.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require small employers to allow eligible employees to participate in payroll deposit individual retirement accounts.
Expands eligibility for the tax credit for retirement savings contributions.
Document 10: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act to revise the formula for determining transfers to the Multiemployer Health Benefit Plan.
Requires consideration of beneficiaries enrolled in the Plan as of the enactment of this Act.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of U.S. foreign policy, particularly focusing on sanctions and legislative measures related to countries like Iran, Sudan, and Syria.

1: promote the divestment of investments in Iran or the Sudan
2: ensure that only government personnel provide security services at U.S. diplomatic or consular missions in Iraq
requires certain reports to Congress regarding such contracts
3: U.S. policy to assist the government of Ukraine
impose three or more specified sanctions against entities related to Syria
4: prohibits heads of the Department of Homeland Security from obligating or expending funds
waives such prohibition in cases where the prohibition would be inconsistent with the public interest
5: prohibits providing assistance to a country that withdraws from the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons
seeks the return of any material transferred to the country
6: authorize the President to furnish assistance for humanitarian activities in Syria
support efforts for a peaceful resolution of the conflict in Syria
7: requires managers of federal and private pension plans to notify investors
prohibits future investment in any entity included on the list
8: U.S. should continue to support diplomatic efforts in the IAEA
empowered with the explicit authority to impose additional sanctions
9: directs the Department of State to submit to Congress a joint 10-year strategy to counter Iranian activities
provide assistance to ensure Israel's qualitative military edge
10: extends the Terrorism Insurance Program until December 31, 2024
requires reports on findings of the President's Working Group on Financial Markets

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at providing support, benefits, and mental health services to veterans and their families.

Document 1: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation.
Addresses compensation owed for mental health issues related to combat.
Document 2: Makes any member of the Armed Forces eligible for readjustment counseling and related mental health services.
Directs the Secretary of Defense to award grants for emotional support services for family members.
Document 3: Sets maximum reimbursement for states administering veterans' education benefits.
Includes veterans of the Global War on Terror within an ongoing veterans' unemployment study.
Document 4: Recodifies educational assistance programs for members of the reserve components.
Increases monthly educational assistance benefit amounts.
Document 5: Extends authorized concurrent receipt of disability severance pay and compensation for combat-related disabilities.
Makes amendments effective for all veterans with a combat-related disability.
Document 6: Adds Parkinson's disease to the list of diseases considered incurred by military service in Vietnam.
Recognizes diseases manifesting in veterans due to service-related exposure.
Document 7: Expands authority to accept travel benefits for members of the Armed Forces during leave.
Includes benefits for humanitarian support to members receiving care.
Document 8: Amends Family and Medical Leave Act to support family members of deployed Armed Forces members.
Entitles employees caring for veterans undergoing medical treatment to leave.
Document 9: Directs award of the Purple Heart to members killed or wounded in the Fort Hood attack.
Deems members to have been killed or wounded in a combat zone due to an enemy act.
Document 10: Prohibits denial of employment benefits for family members caring for servicemembers undergoing treatment.
Includes protections for covered family members during their absence from work.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving health care access, services, and protections for specific populations, including Native Hawaiians, older Americans, children, and individuals with chronic conditions.

Document 1: Reauthorizes for FY2011-FY2016 and revises the Native Hawaiian Health Care Improvement Act.
Requires any Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) grant to or contract with Papa Ola Lokahi to support community-based initiatives.
Document 2: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 (OAA) to require the Office of Long-Term Care Ombudsman Programs to collect and analyze best practices related to responding to elder abuse.
Develop a consumer-friendly tool to assist older individuals and their families in choosing home and community-based long-term care services.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to accept the results of a state licensure survey.
Extends the period during which a group health plan is a primary payer for ESRD patients.
Document 4: Establishes an Elder Justice program under title XX (Block Grants to States for Social Services).
Directs the Secretary to make grants to eligible entities to establish stationary and mobile forensic centers.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide a positive incentive for hospitals to lower their excess readmission ratios.
Recognizes telehealth services and remote patient monitoring in the national pilot program on payment bundling.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a business-incentive grant program to provide child care through public-private partnerships.
Requires the Secretary to make such grants to states, on a competitive basis, to enable businesses and consortia to carry out certain activities.
Document 7: States that it is the purpose of this title to provide dependable and stable funding for children's health insurance under titles XXI and XIX of the Social Security Act.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make performance bonus payments to states to offset the additional Medicaid and CHIP child enrollment costs.
Document 8: Declares the sense of Congress that colorectal cancer screening tests should be included as part of any basic benefit package.
Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to states and Indian tribes for colorectal health programs.
Document 9: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) to increase civil money penalties, criminal fines, and prison sentences for fraud and abuse under the SSA title XVIII program.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to submit to Congress annual fraud reports with respect to Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program.

Requires the Secretary to establish the Public Health Workforce Loan Repayment Program to provide for the repayment of loans incurred by individuals in the pursuit of the relevant public health workforce educational degree.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the support and recognition of veterans and their families in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay a $300 plot allowance to a state for spouses and children of certain veterans who are buried in state cemeteries.
assistance for families of veterans
Document 3: Includes podiatrists within the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) definition of physician
increases their pay grade to match other VA physician compensation
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a national cemetery in eastern Nebraska
to serve the needs of veterans and their families in eastern Nebraska and western Iowa
Document 10: Directs the President to issue an annual proclamation
in honor of the service and sacrifice of veterans throughout the history of the nation

topic.name: Housing and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to housing finance, insurance, and consumer protection in the mortgage industry.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act (NHA) to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for the first 11 years of the term of an insured mortgage
Increases from 1.55% to 2.05% the 30-year annual premium for an insured mortgage whose original principal obligation exceeds 95% of the remaining principal balance.
Document 2: Reestablishes the NARAB without contingent conditions as a nonprofit corporation to prescribe, on a multi-state basis, licensing and insurance producer qualification requirements
Retains states' regulatory authority over licensing, supervision, disciplining, and setting of licensing fees for insurance producers.
Document 4: Amends the Indian Child Protection and Family Violence Prevention Act to prohibit the final approval of any foster care placement or a foster care license from being issued until the tribal social services agency completes a criminal records check
Defines a 'covered individual' as an adult and any other individual the tribe determines is subject to a criminal records check.
Document 5: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages
Makes employers who violate sex discrimination prohibitions liable in a civil action for either compensatory or punitive damages.
Document 10: Prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating either a nonjudicial or a judicial foreclosure against a covered mortgagor that is otherwise authorized under state law unless the covered mortgagee has used its best efforts to determine whether the mortgagor is eligible for a qualified loan modification
Declares any sale of property in violation of this Act void.

topic.name: Energy Infrastructure and Policy
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing the security, efficiency, and sustainability of energy infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Power Act to authorize the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to order emergency measures to protect the reliability of the bulk-power system.
Directs FERC to require any owner, user, or operator of the domestic bulk-power system to implement measures to protect the system against specified vulnerabilities.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a competitive grant program to provide assistance to commercial demonstration projects for the capture of carbon emissions.
Establishes an interagency task force to develop regulations for the capture and storage of carbon dioxide.
Document 3: Authorizes federal agencies to participate in programs conducted by utilities that support the use of alternative fueled vehicles.
Agencies may also enter into energy savings performance contracts for fueling or charging infrastructure.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise renewable fuel standards and establish incentives for production of cellulosic ethanol.
Instructs the Secretary of Energy to implement a research and development program for carbon dioxide capture technologies.
Document 5: Extends federal procurement of biobased products and biorefinery development grants.
Establishes a biochar research and demonstration program.
Document 6: Establishes an Office of Freight Planning and Development to guide planning and investments in the national transportation system.
Directs the Secretary to develop a National Freight Transportation Strategic Plan.
Document 7: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to implement programs to develop a standard design for small modular reactors.
Requires the Secretary to select proposals for cooperative agreements through competitive procedures.
Document 8: Instructs the Secretary of Transportation to prescribe average fuel economy standards for automobiles and trucks.
Requires consideration of environmental impacts and national security in setting fuel economy standards.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a renewable and alternative energy infrastructure competitive grant program.
Makes eligible for grants projects for new infrastructure for dispensing renewable or alternative energy.
Document 10: Declares U.S. policy to develop a national surface transportation system that protects the environment.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to develop performance criteria to evaluate federal surface transportation programs.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the regulation, approval, and safety of various pharmaceutical products and food items under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product
Prohibits licensure under this Act of a biological product containing specified agents or toxins
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to repeal provisions restricting the importation of prescription drugs
Requires the Secretary to approve such an application if the drug meets all FFDCA requirements for admission into the United States
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a program to strengthen federal activities for ensuring that commercially distributed seafood meets federal food quality and safety requirements
Allows admittance of individual shipments from that country or exporter on evidence from an inspection or a certified laboratory
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to issue regulations requiring each bottled water manufacturer or distributor to annually prepare a report
Prohibits the distribution of bottled water products not in compliance with this Act
Document 5: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to disqualify from being a 'first applicant' an applicant submitting an abbreviated new drug application
A first applicant that has entered into an agreement not to seek approval of an application or begin marketing at the earliest possible date cannot seek approval
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to extend for FY2014-FY2018 the authority of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to collect generic animal drug user fees
Establishes the total amount of revenue each type of generic user fee shall generate
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop and maintain a list of bulk substances from which drug products may be compounded
Authorizes the compounding of a drug that is a copy of a commercially available drug product if the drug is on the drug shortage list
Document 8: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product
Sets forth provisions governing patent infringement actions related to this Act
Document 9: Directs the Attorney General to immediately allow manufacturers and distributors to store hydrocodone compound products in accordance with the physical security requirements
Requires the Comptroller General to submit a report on the reclassification of hydrocodone products under this Act
Document 10: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish a process for the review and approval of over-the-counter (OTC) sunscreen active ingredients
Requires the Secretary to review any request regarding a sunscreen active ingredient or combination of ingredients that has been in use

topic.name: Health Care Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at defining and protecting health care coverage, access, and rights related to medical treatment, religious beliefs, and insurance provisions.

Document 1: Equitable Access to Care and Health Act or the EACH Act - Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to minimum essential health care coverage requirements
Defines 'medical health care' to mean voluntary health treatment by or supervised by a medical doctor
Document 2: Prosthetics Parity Act of 2008 - Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974

Requires a group health plan that provides both medical and surgical benefits and benefits for prosthetic devices and components to provide prosthetics coverage under terms and conditions that are no less favorable
Document 3: Grieving Families Insurance Protection Act of 2009 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit a health insurance issuer offering health insurance coverage in the individual market from establishing or maintaining rules of eligibility based on any individual's receipt of grief counseling
Reduces the benefits covered or offered for coverage on such basis
Document 4: Health Care Conscience Rights Act - Amends title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to declare that nothing in that title requires an individual to purchase individual health insurance coverage that includes coverage of an abortion
Prohibits discrimination against health professionals or facilities that refuse to participate in abortion-related activities
Document 5: No Obamacare Mandate Act - This bill repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that impose an excise tax on medical devices
Repeals the requirement that individuals obtain minimum essential health care coverage
Document 6: Ensuring Quality Health Care for All Americans Act of 2012 - Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Prohibits a group health plan or a health insurance issuer from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion
Document 7: World's Greatest Healthcare Plan Act of 2016 - This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the requirements for individuals to maintain minimum essential coverage
Establishes an advanceable, refundable health insurance tax credit for taxpayers enrolled in health coverage
Document 8: State Leadership in Health Care Act - Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to allow states to apply for a waiver of specified requirements under PPACA
Permits the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny waivers only under certain conditions
Document 9: Restoring Assistance for Families' and Seniors' Health Expenses Act of 2011 - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that increase the adjusted gross income threshold for claiming the tax deduction for medical expenses
Amends PPACA to treat a high deductible health plan as a qualified health plan
Document 10: Protecting Women's Access to Health Care Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit discrimination against health care entities based on their involvement with abortion services
Defines 'health care entity' to include an individual physician or other health care professional, a hospital, or any other kind of health care facility or organization

topic.name: Veterans and Military Benefits
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to benefits, support, and services for veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: Increases from $20,000 to $30,000 the maximum amount of supplemental insurance provided through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for totally disabled veterans.
Establishes an annual cost-of-living adjustment, based on increases in amounts payable under title II of the Social Security Act.
Document 3: Appropriates funds for FY2010 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force.
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of funds appropriated in this title and in other military construction appropriations Acts.
Document 8: Increases, from 2,700 to 3,000 each fiscal year, the maximum number of participants in independent living and assistance programs for veterans with serious employment handicaps.

Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to fund offices of veterans affairs at eligible institutions of higher learning for veterans' certification, outreach, recruitment, and special education programs.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Measures
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on plastic mesh for filters.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures containing n-butyl-1,2-benzisothiazolin-3-one.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain plastic fittings composed of perfluoroalkoxy (PFA) resin.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on ultra-high molecular weight polyethylene yarn.
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on battery-operated, portable personal device for mosquito repellent.
Document 6: provide for the temporary suspension of duty on certain catalytic converter mats.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on calcium chloride phosphate.
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on certain electric dispensing blenders.
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on e-caprolactone polymer.
Document 10: suspend temporarily the duty on scented oil dispensing appliances.

topic.name: Health Research and Awareness
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing research, awareness, and treatment coordination for various health conditions and diseases.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to conduct, support, and promote a comprehensive public education campaign to increase awareness regarding congenital heart disease.
Authorizes the Secretary to award a grant to one entity to enhance and expand infrastructure to track the epidemiology of congenital heart disease.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to develop mechanisms to coordinate NIH paralysis research and rehabilitation activities.
Authorizes such consortia to conduct basic, translational, and clinical paralysis research.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to expand, intensify, and coordinate eating disorder research.
Establishes the Interagency Eating Disorders Coordinating Council within the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).
Document 4: Establishes within the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) the National Diabetes Clinical Care Commission to evaluate and make recommendations regarding better coordination of federal programs related to diabetes.
Developing and testing appropriate methods for outreach and dissemination of educational resources related to diabetes prevention and treatments.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to carry out an education and information campaign to promote stroke prevention.
Requires the Secretary to evaluate the campaign and measure its impact every two years.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS Secretary) to establish a demonstration program to award grants to develop an autism navigator program.
Requires the HHS Secretary to provide for evaluations to determine the effects of navigator services.
Document 7: Amends title V of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants for mandatory reporting to the state department of health by health care providers of pregnancy-related deaths.
Develop uniform definitions of severe maternal morbidity, a research plan, and possible data collection protocols.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convene a Patient-Centered Health Care and Quality of Life Stakeholder Strategic Summit.
Requires the Director to award competitive grants to develop new and expand existing information, resources, and communication materials about symptom management.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for the cost of operating nurse-managed health clinics that provide primary care for underserved populations.
Requires a grant application to contain assurances that a clinic will provide all required comprehensive primary health services.
Document 10: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Cavernous Angioma Clinical Care, Awareness, Research, and Education Center of Excellence.
Develop the Center as a model and training facility for other facilities throughout the United States.

topic.name: Tribal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, restoration, and governance of tribal lands and resources for federally recognized tribes.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of the Interior to retire any coal preference right lease application
designates approximately 7,242 acres of land as the Ah-shi-sle-pah Wilderness
Document 2: modifies Interior's process to make it similar to processes used by the Department of Health and Human Service's Indian Health Service
directs Interior to negotiate contracts and funding agreements in good faith
Document 3: prohibits the Secretary of Energy from using the power of eminent domain
must be sited upon either an existing federal right-of-way or upon federal land
Document 4: repeal a provision prohibiting the Alabama-Coushatta Tribe of Texas from conducting gaming activities
deems any claim by or on behalf of the Tribe against the United States to be extinguished
Document 5: directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a loan program to assist the Crow Tribe
requires the Tribe to manage such land and interests in accordance with a land management program
Document 6: makes the Indian Reorganization Act applicable to all federally recognized Indian tribes
overrules the Supreme Court's decision in Carcieri v. Salazar
Document 7: requires the Secretary of Agriculture to accept offers for land exchanges
applies provisions of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act
Document 8: requires the Secretary of the Interior to hold land in trust for the Tribe
instructs the Secretary to cause the patent to be recorded and issued
Document 9: amends the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act to limit the Indian land eligible for gaming
requires joint approval by the legislature of a state and its governor
Document 10: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to make annual disbursements to fund energy development
authorizes tribes to lease lands held in trust for various periods

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign financing and enhancing transparency in political contributions and expenditures.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements.
Establishes the Fair Elections Review Commission.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives to $500,000 during the election cycle.
Provides for indexing such amounts for inflation.
Document 3: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require that quarterly and annual reports disclose total political expenditures.
Requires such disclosures to include the name and political party affiliation of each candidate in support of whom or in opposition to whom a political expenditure was made.
Document 4: Amends the Stop Trading on Congressional Knowledge Act of 2012 to ensure financial disclosure forms filed by Members of Congress are made available to the public.
Extends the requirement for public availability of financial disclosure forms for executive branch employees.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to permit candidates for election to federal office to designate individuals for fund disbursement in the event of the candidate's death.
Prescribes procedures for filing and revoking such a designation.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to permit candidates for election to federal office to designate individuals for fund disbursement in the event of the candidate's death.
Permits the filing of a designation to contain the candidate's instructions regarding the disbursement of funds.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the deadline for filing subchapter S election for corporate taxpayers.
Allows taxpayers to make a subchapter S election on their current tax return.
Document 9: Prohibits recipients of emergency economic assistance from using such funds for lobbying expenditures or political contributions.
Requires recipients of such funds to file quarterly reports and certifications with the Secretary.
Document 10: Imposes a moratorium on federal agencies taking any regulatory rulemaking action.
Requires the OMB Director to conduct a review of each rule that became effective after October 1, 1991.

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on federal legislative acts that authorize appropriations and outline policies related to defense, education, and victim advocacy.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2009
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate
Directs the Secretary of Defense, acting through the Director of the Office, to require DOD policies for victim assistance
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise HEA programs and to extend the authorization of appropriations for them through FY2013
Adds definitions of a critical foreign language, distance education, and poverty line
Document 4: Reauthorizes through FY2019 the programs under titles I-IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Expresses the sense of Congress that states and local educational agencies (LEAs) retain the rights and responsibilities of determining educational curricula
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles
Makes amounts authorized under this Subtitle available for programs, projects, and activities
Document 6: Amends provisions of the Higher Education Act of 1965 known as the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act
Requires institutions of higher education (IHEs) that participate in title IV (Student Assistance) programs to include in their annual campus security reports
Document 7: This bill authorizes the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide training at state and major urban area fusion centers
DHS shall notify Congress of the number of employees of state, local, tribal, and territorial governments with security clearances sponsored by DHS
Document 8: Reauthorizes and amends authorities relating to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Commissioned Officer Corps
Directs the Secretary to prescribe distribution percentages applicable to grades of lieutenant, lieutenant (junior grade), and ensign
Document 9: Amends provisions of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) known as the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy
Requires IHEs to provide new students and employees with a statement that identifies domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking as crimes
Document 10: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
Requires states to adopt college and career ready academic content standards in reading or language arts and mathematics by the end of 2013

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments to the Medicare and Medicaid programs aimed at improving healthcare services, payment methodologies, and provider coverage.

Document 1: payments of direct graduate education costs under title XVIII (Medicare)
educate residents displaced from a graduate medical education program
Document 2: Medicare payment rates for frequently reimbursed hospital inpatient and outpatient procedures
publicly available on the official Medicare Internet site
Document 3: reduce a hospital's otherwise applicable resident limit
redistribution of residency slots after a hospital closes
Document 4: coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B
amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Document 5: Medicare payment for ventilator-dependent patients
acute care hospitals
Document 6: enhanced matching payments to states
meaningful use of certified health information technology
Document 7: payment for home health services to Medicare beneficiaries
nurse practitioner and clinical nurse specialist
Document 8: Medicaid program to reimburse emergency care
stabilize an emergency medical condition
Document 9: coverage of marriage and family therapist services under Medicare
develop discharge plans for post-hospital services
Document 10: coverage of nursing care services in skilled nursing facilities
24-hour licensed nursing service care

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Compliance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, transparency, and compliance of financial institutions and markets in the United States.

Document 1: Mutual Fund Transparency Act of 2009 - Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the Investment Company Act of 1940 to require brokers and dealers of mutual funds to disclose in writing the related commissions they receive
require a mutual fund company to have a committee composed solely of 'non-interested persons' responsible for selecting nominees for election to the board
Document 2: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to study jointly the appropriate capital requirements for mortgage servicing assets
Prohibits regulations to implement the Global Regulatory Framework for More Resilient Banks and Banking Systems
Document 3: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions
Prescribes minimum leverage ratios (6% of average total consolidated assets) and balance sheet leverage ratios (6% of tier 1 capital for all outstanding balance sheet liabilities)
