Document 4: Amends the Revised Statutes of the United States to authorize the Comptroller of the Currency to charter mutual national banks
Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to authorize a mutual depository to issue Mutual Investment Certificates
Document 5: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs)
Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving, destroying, or affecting any privilege
Document 6: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to exclude from the meaning of the term 'swap' any agreement, contract, or transaction that would otherwise be included as a 'swap'
Requires that such exempted agreements, contracts, or transactions be reported to either a swap data repository
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor from using any adverse information concerning any consumer as the basis for increasing any annual percentage rate of interest
Requires such limitation on the use of adverse information to be clearly and conspicuously described to the consumer
Document 8: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to require rules of the SEC to permit an insured depository institution to elect voluntarily not to provide otherwise mandatory certifications
Establishes conditions under which a mutual holding company may contribute stock to a charitable foundation
Document 9: Prohibits a federal banking agency from formally or informally suggesting, requesting, or ordering a depository institution to terminate either a specific customer account
The 'material reason' criterion shall be satisfied if an agency believes that a specific customer or group of customers poses a threat to national security
Document 10: Directs each lending institution subject to specified interest rate requirements under the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to designate an employee as a compliance officer
Requires lending institutions with annual assets of at least $10 billion to maintain a toll-free telephone number

topic.name: Coast Guard and Maritime Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts focused on the authorization, funding, and operational framework of the U.S. Coast Guard and related maritime safety and environmental protection measures.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2013-FY2014 appropriations for the Coast Guard for: operation and maintenance; acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of navigation aids...
Designates amounts from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund to carry out specified provisions of the Oil Pollution Act of 1990.
Document 2: Authorizes FY2015 appropriations for the Coast Guard for: operation and maintenance; the acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation...
Reduces from 7,200 to 6,000 the number of commissioned officers on the active duty promotion list, subject to current exceptions.
Document 10: Authorizes FY2015-FY2016 appropriations for the Coast Guard for: operation and maintenance; acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation...
Sets forth the skills and qualifications necessary for an individual to be assigned as a waterways operations manager or a facility safety and security specialist.

topic.name: Childhood Hunger and Education
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to address childhood hunger and improve educational access and outcomes for disadvantaged populations.

Document 1: promote the reduction or elimination of childhood hunger
improve the coordination of federal, state, and community resources and services aimed at eliminating food insecurity and hunger.
Document 2: facilitate the inclusion of Indian tribes in the school improvement process
assist tribes in providing education and other services to Indian youth in correctional facilities.
Document 5: expand and enhance partnerships with institutions of higher education
provide need-based grants for college access and increased college persistence to eligible low-income students.
Document 7: provide funding to HUD to expand the Healthcare and Housing (H2) Systems Integration Initiative
address the unmet needs of homeless populations in jurisdictions with the highest need.
Document 6: provide assistance for agricultural rehabilitation and vocational training for veterans
targeted at beginning, socially disadvantaged, transitioning, or legal immigrant farmers and ranchers.

topic.name: Corporate Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to corporate taxation, including amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that affect corporate inversions, tax credits for research and job creation, and the implementation of national sales tax.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to set forth rules governing corporate inversions
requires payment of tax on the deferred overseas profits of U.S. multinational corporations
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the rate of the alternative simplified tax credit for research expenses
make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers a credit against payroll tax liability for a payroll increase
sets the amount of such credit at 20%
Document 4: extends through 2013 the terminating date for provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001
amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2013 the increased American Opportunity tax credit
Document 5: repeals the income tax, employment tax, and estate and gift tax
imposes a national sales tax on the use or consumption in the United States
Document 6: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax credit for social security taxes paid for employee cash tips
requires employers of cosmetologists to report income and tips
Document 7: repeals the income tax, employment tax, and estate and gift tax
imposes a national sales tax on the use or consumption in the United States
Document 8: amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny preferential tax rates for dividends paid by a foreign corporation
revises the definition of 'qualified foreign corporation'
Document 9: amends the Internal Revenue Code to eliminate certain payment requirements for submitting offers-in-compromise of tax liability
expand requirements for electronic filing of tax returns
Document 10: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2012 the period for issuing Build America Bonds
allows financing of levees and other flood control projects

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax regulations and benefits for different sectors and activities.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow the issuance of tax-exempt small issue bonds
to finance the purchase of property or land used primarily for the processing of agricultural products.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the special depreciation rules
for property used predominantly within an Indian reservation.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend until December 31, 2016, the designation period
for certain tax-favored empowerment zones.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce from $18 to $9
the per-barrel tax on beer.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a 20-year depreciation recovery period
for heating, ventilation, air conditioning, or commercial refrigeration systems.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to remove certain limitations
on the tax deduction for employee use of cellular telephones.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the new markets tax credit
and provide for an inflation adjustment to the limitation amount.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the first-time homebuyer tax credit
for members of the uniformed services.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax deduction for charitable contributions
to agricultural research organizations.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the exemption from the alternative minimum tax
for individual taxpayers in 2008.

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks for Trade and Safety
topic.description: This cluster addresses various regulatory acts that govern trade, consumer safety, and environmental standards in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Energy (DOE) from issuing any new loan guarantee of an innovative energy project
Requires DOE also, if it makes a guarantee inconsistent with that written analysis, to give certain congressional committees...
Document 2: Treats as a banned hazardous substance under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act (FHSA) any children's product...
Requires periodic review and, when technologically feasible, more stringent limits.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish requirements to facilitate the tracing of drug products through the pharmaceutical supply distribution chain.
Requires drug manufacturers to provide transaction documentation before, or at the time of, each transfer of ownership of a product...
Document 4: Amends the Jenkins Act to revise provisions governing the collection of taxes on, and trafficking in, cigarettes and smokeless tobacco.
Imposes additional information reporting requirements on such sellers.
Document 5: Establishes U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) as the U.S. Customs and Border Protection Agency (CBPA)
Directs the CBPA Commissioner and the Director of ICE to develop jointly and submit to Congress a Joint Strategic Plan...
Document 6: Establishes the Citrus Disease Research and Development Trust Fund, consisting of revenues from duties paid on imported citrus or citrus products
Makes Fund amounts available to the Secretary of Agriculture to develop a coordinated program of research...
Document 7: Specifies U.S. objectives regarding international insurance standards.
The United States may not agree to, accept, establish, enter into, or consent to the adoption of a final international insurance standard...
Document 8: Extends through FY2025 the duty-free treatment of the products of beneficiary sub-Saharan African countries
The President must not terminate the designation of a country as a beneficiary sub-Saharan African country unless...
Document 9: Establishes U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) as the U.S. Customs and Border Protection Agency (CBPA)
Requires the DHS Secretary to consult with specified congressional committees before negotiating...
Document 10: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund, consisting of amounts appropriated for expenses to implement the loan guarantee program
Prohibits the source of payments from the borrower from being a debt obligation that is made or guaranteed by the federal government.

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss the designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals or changes to existing designations.

1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 10721 E Jefferson Ave in Detroit, Michigan, as the 'Mary E. McCoy Post Office Building.'
2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 14 Red River Avenue North in Cold Spring, Minnesota, as the 'Officer Tommy Decker Memorial Post Office.'
3: Designates the United States courthouse located at 101 East Pecan Street in Sherman, Texas, as the 'Paul Brown United States Courthouse.'
4: Amends P.L. 112-108 to change the street number of the post office designated as the 'Specialist Micheal E. Phillips Post Office'.
5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1900 West Gray Street in Houston, Texas, as the 'Hazel Hainsworth Young Post Office Building.'
6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3957 2nd Avenue in Laurel Hill, Florida, as the 'Sergeant First Class William 'Kelly' Lacey Post Office.'
7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 101 East Market Street in Long Beach, California, as the 'Juanita Millender-McDonald Post Office.'
9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 15 Rochester Street, Bergen, New York, as the Barry G. Miller Post Office.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Programs
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments and programs aimed at addressing social, economic, and environmental issues in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize appropriations for alternative water source projects.
Requires consideration of public water system service areas in grant-making.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to implement a program to prevent fraudulent use of taxpayer identification numbers.
Requires comparison of income tax returns filed under the same taxpayer identification number.
Document 3: Repeals a statutory restriction on using DOD facilities for performing abortions.
Document 4: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act to include provision of diapers as covered assistance.
Targets child care providers for children under age three.
Document 5: Directs exemptions for community banks from specified Basel III regulations.
Focuses on banks with consolidated assets of $50 billion or less.
Document 6: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act regarding payments from the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund.
Prohibits payments to states or tribes certified as having completed coal reclamation.
Document 7: Requires specific labeling for imported articles from the West Bank and Gaza Strip.
Mandates inclusion of geographical origin in compliance with the Tariff Act of 1930.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal certain discount authorities for private entities.
Addresses credit accommodations from banking institutions.
Document 9: Authorizes appropriations for mortgage foreclosure mitigation activities.
Focuses on housing counseling initiatives.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to prohibit premium subsidies based on actual market prices.
Targets crop insurance policies at the time of harvest.
